'''Luke Thomas''' (born 19 October 2002) is a [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

He has also spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].

==Background==
Thomas was born in [[Crewe]], [[Cheshire, England]]. He is of [[Welsh people|Welsh]] descent.

==Playing career==
===Early career===
During his childhood, Thomas began playing [[rugby union]] before switching to [[rugby league]] at the age of 11. He played junior rugby league for Latchford Giants before joining the academy at [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Luke Thomas, factfile on Warrington Wolves youngster |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/20145061.luke-thomas-factfile-warrington-wolves-youngster/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=28 October 2022 |date=17 May 2022}}</ref>

===Club career===
In August 2022, Thomas made his Wolves [[Super League]] début against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="bbc">{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62582817 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos v Warrington Wolves |website=BBC Sport |access-date=28 October 2022 |date=19 August 2022}}</ref>

====Widnes Vikings (DR)====
On 29 May 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on [[RFL dual registration|DR]] loan for the remainder of the 2025 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 May 2025|accessdate=30 May 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Warrington Wolves forward makes Championship switch with lengthy move agreed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-forward-makes-championship-switch-with-lengthy-move-agreed}}</ref>

===International career===
In June 2022, he made his senior international debut for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in a 10–34 defeat against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web |title=France Score First Win Under Frayssinous |url=https://www.intrl.sport/news/france-score-first-win-under-frayssinous/ |publisher=International Rugby League |access-date=28 October 2022 |date=19 June 2022}}</ref> He was called up by Wales during the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] as a replacement for the injured [[Ben Evans (rugby league)|Ben Evans]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Warrington Wolves prop comes into Wales squad for World Cup |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23070597.warrington-wolves-prop-comes-wales-squad-world-cup/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=28 October 2022 |date=22 October 2022}}</ref>
